skill.name: Chat UI Components
skill.description: Chat UI building blocks for React/Next.js from ui.inference.sh. Provides components for chat containers, messages, inputs, typing indicators, and avatars for building messaging interfaces.
skill.summary: React and Next.js chat UI components for building modern messaging interfaces with message lists, input handling, and streaming support.
skill.author: inference-sh-9

security_audit.summary: This is a prompt-only skill containing only documentation and code examples for UI components. No executable code, scripts, or network requests are present. Static analysis detected 0 files and 0 potential security issues. The skill provides React/Next.js chat components from ui.inference.sh which are safe for use in AI code generation workflows.

content.value_statement: Creating chat interfaces from scratch requires complex state management and styling. This skill provides pre-built chat components including containers, messages, inputs, and typing indicators that integrate seamlessly with React and Next.js.
actual_capabilities: Generate ChatContainer component for wrapping chat interfaces
Create ChatMessage components with user, assistant, and system role variants
Build ChatInput component with submit handling and loading states
Add TypingIndicator for showing AI response progress
Integrate with shadcn/ui and Tailwind CSS styling
Support streaming responses in chat interfaces
limitations: Only provides UI components, requires backend integration for actual messaging
Components are React/Next.js specific, not framework-agnostic
Does not include actual AI or LLM integration, only the UI layer

use_cases.title: Build AI Assistant Chat Interface
use_cases.description: Create a chat interface for an AI assistant using Claude or other LLMs with proper message bubbles, input field, and typing indicator.
use_cases.target_user: Web developers building AI chatbot applications

use_cases.title: Add Messaging to Web App
use_cases.description: Add real-time messaging capability to any React or Next.js application with pre-styled components.
use_cases.target_user: Full-stack developers adding customer support chat

use_cases.title: Prototype Chat Features
use_cases.description: Quickly prototype chat-based features in design sprints or MVP development without writing UI code.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Chat Interface
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a basic chat interface using the chat-ui skill components. Include a ChatContainer, a few ChatMessage examples with different roles, and a ChatInput with placeholder text.

prompt_templates.title: AI Chat with Typing Indicator
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a complete AI chat interface using chat-ui components. Include ChatContainer, ChatMessage for user and assistant, a ChatInput that calls handleSend on submit, and a TypingIndicator that shows when isLoading is true.

prompt_templates.title: Chat with Custom Styling
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a chat interface using chat-ui components with custom Tailwind CSS classes. Style the ChatMessage components with different backgrounds for user vs assistant messages, and customize the ChatInput appearance.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-Message Chat Thread
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a chat thread using chat-ui components that displays a conversation history. Map through an array of message objects and render ChatMessage components with the appropriate role (user/assistant) and content for each.

output_examples.input: Create a basic chat interface with chat-ui skill
output_examples.output: A React component with ChatContainer wrapping ChatMessage components for user and assistant roles, plus a ChatInput with placeholder. The messages display in a scrollable container with proper styling.

output_examples.input: Build an AI chat with streaming responses
output_examples.output: A functional chat component that shows user messages immediately, displays a TypingIndicator while waiting for the AI response, and renders the assistant message once received. Includes proper loading state management.

best_practices: Always wrap chat components in ChatContainer for proper layout and scrolling
Use the role prop correctly: 'user' for right-aligned messages, 'assistant' for left-aligned
Include the TypingIndicator during API calls to provide visual feedback to users
anti_patterns: Do not use ChatMessage outside of ChatContainer - it will not have proper styling context
Avoid disabling the ChatInput without showing a loading state - users will think the app is frozen
Do not forget to handle the onSubmit callback - the input will not function

faq.question: What is chat-ui skill?
faq.answer: Chat-ui is a skill that provides React/Next.js chat UI components from ui.inference.sh. It includes ChatContainer, ChatMessage, ChatInput, and TypingIndicator components for building chat interfaces.

faq.question: Do I need shadcn/ui to use these components?
faq.answer: Yes, the chat-ui components are designed to work with shadcn/ui and require Tailwind CSS. Install them using: npx shadcn@latest add https://ui.inference.sh/r/chat.json

faq.question: Can I use these components with frameworks other than React?
faq.answer: No, the chat-ui components are React-specific. They require React and Next.js to function properly.

faq.question: How do I handle streaming responses?
faq.answer: Use the TypingIndicator component to show a loading state while receiving streaming responses. Once a chunk arrives, replace the indicator with a ChatMessage component.

faq.question: Are these components accessible?
faq.answer: Yes, the components follow shadcn/ui accessibility standards and include proper ARIA attributes and keyboard navigation support.

faq.question: Can I customize the styling?
faq.answer: Yes, all components accept a className prop that merges with default Tailwind classes. You can override colors, spacing, and other styling as needed.
